content: I need to rework my budget. I'm a 43-year-old AI ethics researcher at a think tank in DC, and my back pain is getting worse. I might need to cut back on work hours soon.
I'm sorry to hear about your back pain. What's your current income and what are your major expenses?
I make $92,000 a year. Rent in my Columbia Heights apartment is $2,400 monthly. I've been seeing a chiropractor twice a week at $75 a visit.
That's a significant amount going to healthcare. Have you explored other treatment options or insurance coverage?
My insurance covers 70% of the chiropractic, so I'm paying $45 per visit. I'm also looking into physical therapy, which would be $60 a session after insurance.
It sounds like healthcare is a major expense. What about your other interests? You mentioned urban sketching and marathon running?
Yeah, I spend about $80 a month on sketching supplies - I just bought a $300 portable easel last month. For running, I've got a $180 monthly gym membership that includes physical therapy services.
That's quite an investment in fitness. Have you considered outdoor running or home workouts to save money?
I've tried, but my back pain makes outdoor running difficult. I'm training for a marathon in November, so I need the specialized equipment and guidance.
That makes sense. What about your hiking hobby? Any costs associated with that?
I just bought new hiking boots for $220 last month. I usually take one big hiking trip each year - this year I'm planning a week in the White Mountains, which will cost about $1,200 total.
Those are some significant one-time expenses. How are you managing your savings and retirement contributions?
I've been putting 15% of my income into my 401(k), but I'm thinking about reducing it to 10% to free up cash for potential medical treatments. I've got about $45,000 saved so far.
That's a tough decision. Have you looked into any alternative therapies or pain management techniques that might be more cost-effective?
I'm considering acupuncture - it's $65 a session after insurance. I'm also thinking about moving to a cheaper neighborhood to reduce my rent by about $600 a month.
Moving could definitely help your budget. What areas are you considering?
I'm looking at Petworth or Brightwood. I could get a similar apartment for around $1,800. That would save me $7,200 a year.
That's a significant saving. With your back pain and potential work reduction, it might be worth making that change sooner rather than later.
I agree. I'm also thinking about picking up some freelance AI ethics consulting work, which could bring in an extra $1,500-2,000 a month if I can manage the hours.
